title: horkelia tweedyi
intro: Horkelia tweedyi, also known as the Sierra horkelia, is a perennial wildflower native to the Sierra Nevada mountain range in California, USA. It is a member of the saxifrage family and is characterized by its pink to purple flowers and linear, lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and preventing infections. Horkelia tweedyi can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its medicinal properties have been traditionally used to support overall health and well-being, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and promoting immune function.
